name: 50 Year Mortgage System Dynamics
description: This model explores the dynamics introduced by offering 50-year mortgage terms, impacting housing affordability, demand, and pricing.

nodes.label: Availability of 50yr Mortgage
nodes.desc: Policy or market decision to offer 50-year mortgage terms, influencing borrowing options.
nodes.example: In 2025, a policy change allowed banks to offer 50-year mortgages, expanding their loan portfolio.

nodes.label: Average Mortgage Term
nodes.desc: The average length of mortgage terms, which increases with the introduction of 50-year options.
nodes.example: After 50-year loans became available, the average mortgage term increased from 30 to 35 years.

nodes.label: Monthly Mortgage Payment
nodes.desc: The typical monthly payment required for new mortgages, reduced by extended terms.
nodes.example: Switching from a 30-year to a 50-year mortgage reduced monthly payments from $2,000 to $1,500.

nodes.label: Perceived Housing Affordability
nodes.desc: Potential buyers' perception of affordability, often based on monthly payments.
nodes.example: With lower monthly payments, more renters felt they could afford a home in 2026.

nodes.label: Demand for Housing
nodes.desc: The number of buyers looking to purchase homes, influenced by affordability perceptions.
nodes.example: When monthly payments decreased, the number of active home buyers increased by 15%.

nodes.label: Housing Prices
nodes.desc: Market value of homes, influenced by demand levels and perceived affordability.
nodes.example: In a competitive market, increased demand pushed housing prices up by 20% over two years.

nodes.label: Interest Rates
nodes.desc: The cost of borrowing money, which can change independently but affects mortgage terms and payments.
nodes.example: A reduction in interest rates from 4% to 3% decreased overall mortgage costs.

loops.name: Affordability-Demand Growth
loops.desc: Increased affordability from lower monthly payments encourages more buyers, raising demand and potentially prices.

loops.name: Price-Feedback Loop
loops.desc: As housing prices rise, perceived affordability decreases, which can temper demand growth.

archetypes.name: Balancing Process with Delay
archetypes.description: The balancing feedback loop with delayed effect regulates rapid demand spikes due to perceived affordability.
